programme.title: Beyond Belief: Fact or Fiction
programme.desc: This episode features the following cases: The Mystery of Douglas Hibbard, Wheelchair Man, The Vigil, The Mandarin's Bowl, Ghost Writer.

programme.title: Beyond Belief: Fact or Fiction: Episode 8
programme.desc: This episode features the following cases: Caitlin's Candle, The Flower Jury, The Mentor, The Old Bike, The Music Teacher.

programme.title: Beyond Belief: Fact or Fiction: Episode 6
programme.desc: This episode features the following cases: The Candlestick, The Diner, From the Agency, The Magic Rose Garden, The Jeep.

programme.title: Beyond Belief: Fact or Fiction: Episode 3
programme.desc: This episode features the following cases: The Wall, The Chalkboard, The Getaway, The Prescription, Summer Camp.

programme.title: Beyond Belief: Fact or Fiction
programme.desc: This episode features the following cases: The Hand, The Battered Doll, Poker Justice, Above the Clouds, Screen Saver.

programme.title: Beyond Belief: Fact or Fiction
programme.desc: This episode features the following cases: Moonstruck Beach, Healing Hands, Aspen Sunny Side, Night Walker, Hot Car.

programme.title: Beyond Belief: Fact or Fiction: Episode 7
programme.desc: This episode features the following cases: The Mummy, The Perfect Record, Grave Sitting, Murder on the Second Floor, They Towed My Car.

programme.title: Beyond Belief: Fact or Fiction: Episode 3
programme.desc: This episode features the following cases: Out of Service, When I Was Big, The Greedy Investor, Seven Hours of Bad Luck, The Secret of the Coins.

programme.title: Beyond Belief: Fact or Fiction: Episode 1
programme.desc: This episode features the following cases: The Apparition, The Electric Chair, On the Road, Number One with a Bullet, Dream House.

programme.title: One Step Beyond: Dead Ringer
programme.desc: A woman called Esther has unsettling visions of buildings set alight by an arsonist and claims that her sister, whom she has not seen for many years, is responsible.

programme.title: One Step Beyond: The Stone Cutter
programme.desc: Elderly Simon Lockhart is convinced that he will die in just a few days, because a mysterious stone cutter has already chiseled that date onto his tombstone.

programme.title: One Step Beyond: Make Me Not a Witch
programme.desc: A farm girl's parents are terrified when their daughter reveals that she can read minds. But a priest knows that her gifts can save two young lives.

programme.title: One Step Beyond: The Hand
programme.desc: In a jealous rage, a piano player at a run-down dive murders a beautiful young woman. Although he at first seems to have covered his tracks well enough, he soon discovers that, no matter how hard he tries, he cannot get the woman's blood off his hands.

programme.title: One Step Beyond: Earthquake
programme.desc: A hotel bellboy's premonitions of impending catastrophe are ignored by a group of well-to-do opera patrons.

programme.title: One Step Beyond: The Forests of the Night
programme.desc: Three friends on a hunting trip idling away a rainy day stumble upon a mysterious Chinese game that promises to make a man what he is not, such as a mouse a tiger. After playing the game, a mild-mannered member of the trio begins acting strangely.

programme.title: One Step Beyond: Call for Tomorrow
programme.desc: A famous actress has a complete breakdown after the death of her young daughter. After she recovers, she returns home, only to be convinced that she can hear her daughter calling to her from beyond the grave.

programme.title: One Step Beyond: Who Are You?
programme.desc: In the 1900s, Laurie Warren, recovering from scarlet fever it was feared would kill her, travels to the home of a Mr and Mrs Mason, whose daughter has recently died and imagines that she is that daughter rejecting her parents when they come after her.

programme.title: One Step Beyond: The Lovers
programme.desc: Evil spirits are determined to break up the romance between Otto, a patron at a Viennese tavern, and Elsa, a beautiful waitress there. But the connection between the two lovers seems to be stronger than the spirits counted on.

programme.title: Dead Cert
programme.desc: A gang of London gangsters get more than they bargained for when a group of businessmen make an offer to buy their club.

programme.title: Mayday
programme.desc: A flight from Los Angeles to London quickly turns into a terror-filled experience as passengers mysteriously start to disappear without a trace.

programme.title: The Wretched
programme.desc: A defiant teenage boy faces off with a thousand year-old witch, who is living beneath the skin of and posing as the woman next door.

programme.title: House of Darkness
programme.desc: Hap (Justin Long) drives Mina (Kate Bosworth) home to her mysterious estate after a night of drinking. Hap gets more than he bargained for when unexpected occurrences offset his attempts at a romantic connection with this mysterious object of his desire.

programme.title: Sorority House Massacre
programme.desc: College student Beth and her sorority sisters are stalked by an escaped psychopathic killer who shares a strange telepathic link with her.

programme.title: Dark Oracle
programme.desc: Evil Sage has taken real Sage's place and Lance and Cally are shocked when this new, uncensored Sage devastates Lance by hooking up with Vern.

programme.title: Dark Oracle
programme.desc: Lance is playing an online role-playing game 24/7 and even Dizzy thinks he's out of control. A staged intervention fails utterly and Lance is back at the keyboard.

programme.title: Dark Oracle
programme.desc: Lance and Cally find themselves the unwitting hosts of a house party that goes completely out of control.

programme.title: Dark Oracle
programme.desc: When the comic predicts that Dizzy will become a rock star, the "Dister" surprises everyone by entering a local "Canadian Idol" type contest.

programme.title: Dark Oracle
programme.desc: When Vern decides to use his expertise in the dark arts to grow his own "familiar", Cally and Lance use the comic clues to find it before the it hatches.

programme.title: Summer of Fear
programme.desc: A teenage girl's life is turned upside down after her cousin moves into her house, and as time goes by she begins to suspect that she may be a practitioner of witchcraft.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Blood, Sweat and Tears
programme.desc: Bob and Margaret decide they need to get fit.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Shopping
programme.desc: Bob and Margaret, concerned at the amount they are spending in take away food and expensive local food shops, decide on an expedition to the Super Save Shopatropolis in Croydon.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Cottage Country
programme.desc: Bob and Margaret get invited up to the cottage of Dr. Yoselheiffer, President of the Canadian Council of Dentists.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Bob Gets Wired
programme.desc: When Margaret forgets to pay for some sprinkles at the local cake shop, she is hauled in as a shoplifter.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Bob's Burglary
programme.desc: While Bob and Margaret are sleeping, the local criminals, Beany and Boney clear their house - TV, video, microwave and electric toothbrush all go.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Neighbours
programme.desc: In this story of neighbourhood social angst, Bob is having a complex burglar alarm installed by Graham, a sad, lonely man whose confidence is lifted immeasurably by this rare sale to Bob.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: The Getaway
programme.desc: Margaret is nominated for the Golden Arch Support award for outstanding service in the field of footcare.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: The Wedding
programme.desc: Cookie and Melvin invite Bob and Margaret to the wedding of their goddaughter, Tiffany.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Stranded in Toronto
programme.desc: When Bob and Margaret take a trip to New York, their flight is re-routed to Toronto due to a storm.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: A Tale of Two Dentists
programme.desc: Bob's business is booming, and when Margaret chips a tooth Bob tells Margaret that he is unable to give her an appointment for sometime.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: The Dental Convention
programme.desc: The time for the annual dental convention has come. Bob is not keen on going when he sees that a colleague from his student days, Bernard Wiggins, a "loud mouthed big shot", is a featured speaker.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: A New Life
programme.desc: At a creepy chiropodist social night, Bob and Margaret encounter a couple who are rapturous about the new planned community where they now live.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Margaret Gets a Job
programme.desc: Bob and Margaret have made the big move to Canada.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Friends for Dinner
programme.desc: Bob and Margaret have been invited to Neil and Moira's for dinner, or so they think.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: The Trouble with Mummy
programme.desc: Bob is concerned when he hears that his mother's best friend Pearl has passed on.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Strangers in a Strange Land
programme.desc: Bob and Margaret are mortified to discover that while they have been cooped up at Cookie and Melvin's house in Mississauga Ontario, their dogs have been left unattended by Penny, Bob's unreliable receptionist.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: No Trouble
programme.desc: This charming, witty animated series focuses on the ordinary lives of a middle class dentist and his foot doctor wife. Together, they tackle traumas as being burgled, going on a holiday and deciding whether or not having a child will ruin their lives.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Gone to Seed
programme.desc: Bob decides to donate his sperm to a local sperm bank.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: A Bob or Two
programme.desc: Bob buys an inexpensive painting from the neighbours at a junk sale (mostly motivated by pity since these neighbours have fallen on hard times) and hangs it in his surgery to brighten things up?

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: A Night In
programme.desc: Bob and Margaret find themselves at loose ends one evening when, to their dismay, they find that there is absolutely nothing to watch on TV.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: I, Bob
programme.desc: When an efficiency expert comes to assess the Inner City Dental Clinic, Bob gets side shifted out of a job.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Outward Bound
programme.desc: Bob joins his male colleagues on a canoe trip to a provincial park.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Party Politics
programme.desc: Margaret runs into a distant friend who asks her to have an Ann Summers-type party in her home.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Mastermind
programme.desc: When Bob and Margaret have a fender-bender with a local mobster, they accidentally turn him into the police.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Driving Bob
programme.desc: Bob and Margaret have put it off for too long. They need to get their Canadian drivers' licenses.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Trick or Treat
programme.desc: It's Halloween. It is also Bob father's birthday and his mother wants to celebrate by visiting his grave. Bob is not comfortable with this and tries to get out of it.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Over-Exposed
programme.desc: Margaret accidentally picks up her neighbour's photos at the pharmacy.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Age Before Beauty
programme.desc: Margaret gets a new hairstyle but Bob doesn't even notice.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: A Very Fishy Christmas
programme.desc: Bob and Margaret experience a truly Canadian Christmas.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Problems
programme.desc: At her clinic one-day, Margaret sees an old school chum, and invites her and her husband Matt to dinner.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Book Club
programme.desc: Bob and Margaret join a book club in the hopes of expanding their tiny social horizon.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: The Fly on the Wall
programme.desc: Feeling belittled by his family, Bob jumps at the chance to be the subject of a fly on the wall documentary.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: My Foot Hurts
programme.desc: Margaret gets her own local call-in radio show entitled My Foot Hurts. On the first day, nervous and short on callers, she mentions her favourite curry house on the air?

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Mummy's Boy
programme.desc: Bobs mother is coming all the way from England to visit him.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: The Candidate
programme.desc: Margaret finds herself inadvertently running for City Council when the candidate she is supporting drops out of the race.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Gary
programme.desc: Bob and Margaret thought they knew everything about their next door neighbours, until Joyce and Trevor introduce them to their grown son, Gary.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Fish at the Bat
programme.desc: Bob gets cajoled into joining his office softball team.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Discomfort of Strangers
programme.desc: Margaret fears that her relationship with Bob is getting stale after they have a petty argument.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Til Death Do Us
programme.desc: Bob gets conned into buying a cemetery plot for Margaret as a surprise 15th wedding anniversary gift.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: The Player
programme.desc: Bob attends a personal finance seminar to help him get his hopeless financial house in order.

programme.title: On the Case: Murder at Bird Key
programme.desc: A successful business man is found brutally murdered in his palatial Southern Florida home. A mysterious backpack found at the crime scene is the key to the case.

programme.title: On the Case: Stabbed in the Heart
programme.desc: A beloved mother of four is stabbed to death in her bathroom. A mysterious v shaped wound on her forehead finally leads police to her killer decades later.

programme.title: On the Case: A Hidden Clue
programme.desc: A hidden clue may hold the key to solving the vicious murder of a young woman.

programme.title: On the Case: Courage and Conviction
programme.desc: A college student's battle for survival may be the key to solving a double homicide from years earlier.

programme.title: On the Case: The Pastor's Wife
programme.desc: Two boys witness the brutal murder of their mother, and their story may bring her cold-blooded killer to justice 30 years later.

programme.title: On the Case: The Day After Christmas
programme.desc: A man and his girlfriend are attacked by a mysterious intruder in their home. Despite near fatal wounds, she survives and helps investigators solve the crime.

programme.title: On the Case: A Secret Meeting
programme.desc: After a teenager mysteriously disappears, police must determine if a secret meeting with a young man led to her murder.

programme.title: On the Case: It Won't End Well
programme.desc: A young woman's strange encounter with a man outside her home becomes the thread that unravels a terrifying murder mystery.

programme.title: On the Case: A Father's Quest
programme.desc: After finding his beautiful 19 year old daughter dead, a father launches a relentless pursuit of her killer.

programme.title: On the Case: Betrayal in the Mountains
programme.desc: A man calls 911, saying his wife had been shot when she walked in on a robbery, and that he had used deadly force on the intruder. The investigation of what seems like an open-and-shut case of self defense reveals the secrets of several past murders.

programme.title: On the Case: Footprints and Whispers
programme.desc: A beloved husband and wife are found dead in their own home. Will the killers own words bring him to justice?

programme.title: On the Case: Tracks of a Killer
programme.desc: Police retrace a young woman's final movements with hope that it will lead them straight to her killer.

programme.title: On the Case: A Heartbreaking Discovery
programme.desc: When a young mother disappears police painstakingly retrace her last known movements. The trail of clues they uncover leads to a shocking and heartbreaking discovery.

programme.title: On the Case: A Drop of Blood
programme.desc: A single mother is murdered at work. Will a single drop of blood lead police to her killer?

programme.title: On the Case: A Mystery on Cherry Hill
programme.desc: When a beautiful young woman vanishes without a trace, it may take another tragedy before police will be able to solve the mystery of her disappearance.

programme.title: On the Case: A Different Time
programme.desc: When Barbara Rowan left her house on an afternoon in 1984, she had a baseball mitt, a ball and a radio. Barbara claimed she was on her way to meet her coach. After she didn't return home, Police discovered that the teenager didn't play any sports.

programme.title: On the Case: From Dusk to Darkness
programme.desc: After a 10-year-old girl mysteriously disappears, her family and the police undergo a tormenting journey to find a lead.

programme.title: On the Case: A Promise Kept
programme.desc: A cold-case detective reopens a decades-old murder case and promises to get justice on behalf of the victim's family.

programme.title: On the Case: As She Lay Dying
programme.desc: August 19th, 2014. It was a hot afternoon in Portland, Oregon when police received a distressing 911-call. A woman had been stabbed at an apartment complex. By the time first responders arrived, the victim had already lost too much blood to be saved.

programme.title: Relic Hunter: Deadline
programme.desc: Allan comes to Sydney, telling he has 12 hours before a Necron virus kills him unless he delivers Tau to a blackmailer who has the antidote.

programme.title: Relic Hunter: Wages of Sydney
programme.desc: Quan Shu, Manchuria 1359 BC: The Chinese invent an explosive gift egg. New York, 2001: A rival beats Sydney and Nigel to a map leading to the Dragon's Egg.

programme.title: Relic Hunter: Vampire's Kiss
programme.desc: Lucas Blackmer, a babe-magnet, professor and a novelist asks Sydney to help find Vlad Tepes' lost relic. And he isn't the only one after it.

programme.title: Farscape: Farscape: S1 E3 - Back And Back And Back To The Future
programme.desc: The crew rescues a scientist and his assistant from a disintegrating ship.
programme.sub-title: Farscape: S1 E3 - Back And Back And Back To The Future

programme.title: Farscape: Farscape: S1 E4 - Throne For A Loss
programme.desc: Rygel is captured and held for ransom by some bounty hunters.
programme.sub-title: Farscape: S1 E4 - Throne For A Loss

programme.title: Farscape: Farscape: S1 E5 - PK Tech Girl
programme.desc: After finding the legendary Peacekeeper battleship, Zelbinion, dead in space Crichton, Aeryn, and D'Argo find a Peacekeeper tech under Crais's command.
programme.sub-title: Farscape: S1 E5 - PK Tech Girl

programme.title: Farscape: Farscape: S1 E6 - Thank God It's Friday, Again
programme.desc: The crew visits a planet full of happy workers. Soon D'Argo and Zhaan start working and refuse to return to the ship.
programme.sub-title: Farscape: S1 E6 - Thank God It's Friday, Again

programme.title: Farscape: Farscape: S1 E7 - I, E.T.
programme.desc: A peacekeeper beacon goes off and Moya has to land on an alien planet to prevent its signal being intercepted and Moya being discovered.

programme.title: Farscape: Farscape: S1 E8 - That Old Black Magic
programme.desc: Crichton's spirit is captured by Maldis. The sorcerer thrives on vengeance and hate, he brings Crais's spirit down. And the two spirits battle.
programme.sub-title: Farscape: S1 E8 - That Old Black Magic

programme.title: The Dead Zone: Big Top
programme.desc: Johnny's visions inadvertently lead him to reexamine a ten-year-old murder case and his current relationship with his teenaged son, JJ.

programme.title: The Dead Zone: Interred
programme.desc: When Johnny has disturbing visions of a man buried alive, he must overcome his differences with Sheriff Turner in the hopes of saving the man's life.

programme.title: The Dead Zone: Switch
programme.desc: After Johnny boards a train for some much-needed relaxation, he has a vision of a woman thrown to her death and soon falls for the intriguing beauty.

programme.title: The Dead Zone: Numb
programme.desc: A serious case of appendicitis sends Johnny into another coma, and this time it's up to Sarah to save his life.

programme.title: The Dead Zone: Outcome
programme.desc: Johnny has to race against the clock to prevent a massive explosion from ripping apart the regional bus station.

programme.title: Starhunter Redux: Supermax Redux (REDUX)
programme.desc: While transporting Quigley, a prisoner who appears to be quite insane, the crew save the lives of an old nemesis and his associates. Max offers to share a stash of Onmium, a mineral rarer than diamonds and worth billions.

programme.title: Starhunter Redux: Pandora's Box (REDUX)
programme.desc: The Trans-Utopian experiences a power drain caused by Keres Station research facility. Not knowing they are the cause, Travis accepts an assignment from their head scientist to capture the Raider Quennell and retrieve a sample of the deadly Tethys Virus.

programme.title: Starhunter Redux: Stitch in Time (REDUX)
programme.desc: While transporting Ritson, a deserter from the Jup-Fed Elite Forces, the Trans-Utopian answers a distress call from a mining platform on the asteroid Ida: a mining platform that should not exist.

programme.title: Starhunter Redux: The Prisoner (REDUX)
programme.desc: Travis and Callie pick up a psychiatric patient with amnesia, who grows to remember Travis and wants him dead. In parallel, the new Orchard leader Tristan Catchpole set his sights on Travis, who may have some of his Cluster genes activated.

programme.title: Starhunter Redux: Kate (REDUX)
programme.desc: After the Trans-Utopian collides with a mine, Percy, unhappy with Caravaggio's recent crisis performance, creates a female Al program with more intuition, but it quickly develops too much independence... and an attraction to Travis.

programme.title: Robbie Coltrane's Critical Evidence: The Double Axe Murderer: Dennis Smalley
programme.desc: A double axe murderer and no clues - except tiny flakes of paint discovered deep in the wounds of one of the victims. This is a story of how forensic science - and the determination of one detective - found the killer.
programme.sub-title: The Double Axe Murderer: Dennis Smalley

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Gemma McCluskie
programme.desc: The harrowing story of EastEnders actress Gemma McCluskie, who was murdered by her older brother in the flat they shared in east London in 2012.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Joanne Dennehy
programme.desc: Dermot Murnaghan returns to uncover the truth behind the horrific crimes of Peterborough serial killer Joanna Dennehy.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Rachel Nickell
programme.desc: A hard-hitting look at the murder of Rachel Nickell, who was sexually assaulted and murdered in front of her two-year-old son on Wimbledon Common in 1992.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Garry Newlove
programme.desc: Garry Newlove’s wife Helen talks about the events on the night of his murder and the days afterwards, as well as her current work as a peer in the House of Lords combatting anti-social behaviour.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Peter Sutcliffe
programme.desc: Arguably Britain's most infamous serial killer, Peter Sutcliffe became known as The Yorkshire Ripper when he began murdering women in Yorkshire during the 1970s.

programme.title: Meet, Marry, Murder: Ricardo Godinho
programme.desc: Aliny and Ricardo Godinho had four children and a relationship history that spanned 17 years. While Aliny put her kids at the centre of everything she did, for Ricardo they were a morbid obsession, and one that would lead to a shocking, brutal crime.

programme.title: Meet, Marry, Murder: Brian Burns
programme.desc: Brian and Carla Burns were both medics in their 40s when they met and fell in love. But married bliss didn’t last, and divorce proceedings were messy. Carla disappeared, and fingers pointed at Brian, but he was ready with a bizarre explanation.

programme.title: Meet, Marry, Murder: Kelly Cochran
programme.desc: When Chris Regan started dating coworker Kelly Cochran, he knew she was married. But he had no idea of the wedding day pact she had made with husband Jason. Chris had become entangled in a love triangle that would see two people lose their lives.

programme.title: Meet, Marry, Murder: Chaz Higgs
programme.desc: High-profile politician Kathy Augustine married nurse Chaz Higgs after a whirlwind romance. They were an unlikely match, and when their marriage hit the rocks, Chaz decided to use his medical training to commit an undetectable crime.

programme.title: Meet, Marry, Murder: Chacey Poynter
programme.desc: Bob Poynter was old enough to be Chacey’s father. And he was married. But those were minor obstacles to her. However, when their romance hit the skids, Chacey enlisted yet another of her lovers to help bring the marriage to a brutal end.

programme.title: Meet, Marry, Murder: Ramanodge Unmathallegadoo
programme.desc: At the age of 16, Sana Muhammad had an arranged marriage to Ramanodge Unmathallegadoo, a man almost twice her age. She managed to escape the unhappy relationship and found new love, but Ramanodge decided she would never get to enjoy her new life.

programme.title: Meet, Marry, Murder: Janbaz Tarin
programme.desc: Raneem Oudeh came to the UK from Syria to escape war, looking forward to a brighter future for herself and her son. Everything was falling into place until she met Janbaz Tarin, a man who saw no boundary between love, obsession, and extreme violence.

programme.title: Meet, Marry, Murder: Keland Hill
programme.desc: Tiffany and Keland Hill were military veterans with a shining service history. But while Tiffany still lived to serve, Keland lived to control, stalk and terrorise his wife. He was extremely violent, but no one predicted his final act of brutality.

programme.title: Meet, Marry, Murder: Denise Williams
programme.desc: Denise Williams was devastated when her husband Mike disappeared on a hunting trip. He was feared drowned. But Mike’s mother Cheryl didn’t buy it, and after years of relentless campaigning, she finally uncovered the truth about her son’s death.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Beverly Allitt
programme.desc: Beverley Allitt was thought to be a 'model' nurse. Very astute, caring and always willing to help, why did she murder four children and attempt to kill 11 others?

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Rochdale Groomers
programme.desc: Rochdale Groomers: Documentary examining the sex abuse scandal which occurred in the North of England and appeared to go undetected for quite some time.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Stephen Port
programme.desc: A chilling look at the crimes of Stephen Port, the east London serial killer who poisoned four young men with lethal doses of a date rape drug.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Peter Tobin
programme.desc: Peter Tobin is one of the most notorious serial killers in the UK. From 1991–2006 he raped and murdered three young girls; Dinah McNicol, Vicky Hamilton and Angelika Kluk. Yet even prior to these horrific murders he had a history of violence.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Angus Sinclair
programme.desc: Angus Sinclair, called Scotland's worst serial killer, is linked to the 1977 murders of two girls in Edinburgh.

programme.title: Weird Or What? With William Shatner: Bullet Proof Boobs, Amazing Coincidences, Directional Cows
programme.desc: ...With William Shatner. Bullet Proof Boobs, Amazing Coincidences, Directional Cows: Cows align with the Earth's magnetic field, and a young makes an incredible connection.
programme.sub-title: Bullet Proof Boobs, Amazing Coincidences, Directional Cows

programme.title: Weird Or What? With William Shatner: Cellular Memory, Ice Circles, Chimera
programme.desc: ...With William Shatner. Cellular Memory, Ice Circles, Chimera: Check out a heart patient's unbelievable transformation, circles of ice in rivers and a shocking DNA test.

programme.title: Weird Or What? With William Shatner: The Hobbit, Limb Generation, Mind Over Matter
programme.desc: ...With William Shatner. The Hobbit, Limb Generation, Mind Over Matter: A look at the future of mind control, an extinct hobbit race and whether humans can regenerate limbs.
programme.sub-title: The Hobbit, Limb Generation, Mind Over Matter

programme.title: Weird Or What? With William Shatner: Alien Encounters
programme.desc: Alien Encounters: A cow is mutilated, a man is repeatedly abducted from his bed, and an astronomer's telescope intercepts a mysterious message.

programme.title: Weird Or What? With William Shatner: Life After Death
programme.desc: Life After Death: Scientists in England conduct groundbreaking séances, while someone returns from the grave.

programme.title: Weird Or What? With William Shatner: Amazing Survival
programme.desc: Amazing Survival: Incredible stories of people who cheated death, including a man who escaped from the avalanche of debris caused by the collapse of the World Trade Center.

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed: Episode 14

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed: Episode 13
programme.desc: In 2011, a vault of US government files about UFO sightings and alien activity was released. This series investigates these documents and re-examines key evidence about UFOs, alien abductions and government cover-ups.

programme.desc: In 2011, a vault of US government files about UFO sightings and alien activity was released. This series investigates these documents and re-examines key evidence about UFOs, alien abductions and government cover-ups.

programme.desc: In 2011, a vault of US government files about UFO sightings and alien activity was released. This series investigates these documents and re-examines key evidence about UFOs, alien abductions and government cover-ups.

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed: Episode 9
programme.desc: We have always been told that mankind enjoys free will. Is this really true or have aliens created the means for controlling humanity?

programme.title: Strangest Things: The Battery, The Screaming Mummy And The Medieval Iron Hand
programme.desc: Is a 2,000-year-old weathered jar discovered on an Iraqi excavation really the world’s oldest battery? Why is a 3,000-year-old Egyptian mummy screaming? Did a metal hand from 15th century Germany belong to some kind of medieval Iron Man?
programme.sub-title: The Battery, The Screaming Mummy And The Medieval Iron Hand

programme.title: Strangest Things: The Ancient Computer, The Pigeon And The Roman Nanotechnology,
programme.desc: Is a corroded lump of bronze found on the sea floor really a 2,000-year-old computer? Was a thousand-pound World War Two bomb really piloted by a pigeon? What is the sub-atomic secret of a Roman colour-changing glass cup?
programme.sub-title: The Ancient Computer, The Pigeon And The Roman Nanotechnology,

programme.title: Strangest Things: German Codes, Egyptian Helicopters And The Future Machine
programme.desc: Is a device that looks like a typewriter in a wooden box really one of the most dangerous weapons in history? Why is there a modern helicopter carved in an ancient Egyptian temple? Can an 800-year-old Arabic machine really calculate your future?
programme.sub-title: German Codes, Egyptian Helicopters And The Future Machine

programme.title: Strangest Things: The Viking Coin, The Ancient Disk And The Dead Girl That Saved A Million Lives
programme.desc: Can a shred of metal dug up on a thousand-year-old native American site really be Viking? Is a strange fired clay disc discovered on Crete really the product of Europe’s earliest civilisation, or is it a fake?
programme.sub-title: The Viking Coin, The Ancient Disk And The Dead Girl That Saved A Million Lives

programme.title: Secrets In The Ice: The Vanishing Of Flight CS-59

programme.desc: Merging advanced scanning technology and scientific research, this series investigates curiosities that have been lost for millennia, or never seen before, exploring how and why they ended up in the most desolate and distant corners of the planet.

programme.title: Moesha: A Concerted Effort (Part 1)

programme.desc: Mo cries the lovesick blues on a phone-in radio show. The DJ, feeling sorry for Mo, gives her four free tickets to an upcoming concert.

programme.desc: Mo is shirking her duties at home and her grades are falling. Frank blames Q.

programme.title: Moesha: Break A Leg
programme.desc: Mo is teaching a dance class to young girls. Myles wants to join because he has a crush on one of the girls.

programme.title: Moesha: Strike A Pose

programme.desc: Moesha wants to throw Hakeem a surprise birthday party and invites his estranged father.

programme.desc: Moesha houses a beautiful French exchange student. Q picks the French girl and Kim to be back-up singers in his new band.

programme.title: Moesha: Prom Fright

programme.desc: Mo has been dating Bridgewood classmate Jeremy since her break up with Q.

programme.title: Inside Royalty: The Clothes Show
programme.desc: Behind the image of Princess Diana as one of the world’s most photographed women is a revealing story of glamour, sex and perfection.

programme.title: Inside Royalty: The Good Life
programme.desc: What happens when a Princess and a Duchess set about spending their husband’s money?

programme.title: Inside Royalty: The Family Jewels
programme.desc: The Queen’s priceless diamonds, sapphires, emeralds, and rubies far outshine any other jewellery collection in the modern world.

programme.title: Kate A Young Queen in Waiting
programme.desc: Kate Middleton, together with her husband Prince William, is leading the British monarchy out of a period of considerable difficulty. She knows that she will one day be Queen. She understands the dignity of that role at the centre of the British nation.

programme.title: Kate Middleton: Working Class to Windsor
programme.desc: Wife, mother, duchess; a journey from working class to Windsor. Kate Middleton’s rise to royalty and influences of international intrigue and admired the world over.

programme.title: Prince William: Destiny
programme.desc: Destiny goes beyond the Royal love affair and explores destiny in relation to the Royal Family.

programme.title: Princes of the Palace
programme.desc: Discover the lives of the British Royal Family's princes and their importance to the Royal House of Windsor. Profiling Princes Phillip, Charles, William, Harry and young Prince George.

programme.title: Princess Diana The Quiet Revolution
programme.desc: No one in history has ever been so universally adored as Diana, Princess of Wales. In her short life she captivated the world with her beauty, charm and limitless compassion.

programme.title: Queen Elizabeth II: Reign Supreme
programme.desc: Reign Supreme is a historical time line, which, chronicles the life of Elizabeth II with a focus on her enduring reign, public image, private life and events since her coronation.

programme.title: Royal Jewels
programme.desc: The Royal Jewels truly represent the mystery and majesty of Monarchy on display, with the Imperial State Crown itself featuring no less than 2,800 diamonds.

programme.title: Royal Secrets: Spare Heirs
programme.desc: The royal children who will never taste the ultimate job - of being a King or Queen of England. From Princess Margaret, who was destined to live and die in her sister's considerable shadow, to Prince Harry, who is making himself universally popular.

programme.title: Royal Secrets: For King and Country
programme.desc: When Edward VIII abdicated, his younger brother, George, suddenly became king and 'inherited' the second world war. The father of Britain's present queen, joined forces with Winston Churchill to stand firm against Adolf Hitler.

programme.title: Royal Secrets: The Man Who Would be King
programme.desc: Prince William is seen as the 'future' of the British monarchy. The heir to the throne who surveys say most people want to see succeed his grandmother, Queen Elizabeth. Why has William become so popular?
programme.sub-title: The Man Who Would be King

programme.title: Royal Secrets: First Steps
programme.desc: Britain's royal babies used to be born and raised behind closed palace gates. Today, they are part and parcel of the commercialisation of Britain's Royal Family. Not so long ago, access to royal babies was restricted and they didn't even go to school

programme.title: Royal Secrets: An Extraordinary Queen
programme.desc: Queen Elizabeth II is the world's oldest monarch and Britain's longest serving Queen. This episode explores the secret of the woman who never expected to become a Queen.

programme.title: Royal Secrets: Edward & Mrs Simpson
programme.desc: The love affair that enthralled the world and brought down a King. The true story of the romance that cost Edward VIII his throne and defined Britain's modern Royal Family.

programme.title: Royal Secrets: Royal Wives
programme.desc: Through the ages they have come to define their Royal husbands behind the scenes. But, in the modern world the royal wives are expected to become part of the family ‘firm'. What is the secret of being a good Royal 'wife'?

programme.title: Royal Secrets: The Royal Mistress
programme.desc: Camilla Parker Bowles was famously referred to as 'the third person' in the marriage of Charles and Diana. Her long standing affair with the heir to the throne made her one of the most famous Royal mistresses in history.

programme.title: Royal Secrets: The People's Princess
programme.desc: Princess Diana became the People's Princess, but her marriage to the heir to the throne, Prince Charles, was a largely loveless affair that saw Diana fighting the royal family she had married into.

programme.title: Royal Secrets: Born to be King
programme.desc: Prince Charles is now the longest 'serving' heir to the British throne in history. He fears that he may never inherit the role he was born to. The Queen's son stands accused of interfering and meddling in British life and the politics of a nation.

programme.title: Royals And Their Pets
programme.desc: The Royal family has always had a very special relationship with their pets: from the lapdog who hid under Mary, Queen of Scots' skirts at her execution to Caesar, King Edward VII's terrier who took precedence over 9 Kings at his masters' State Funeral.

programme.title: Spying on the Royals: Part 1
programme.desc: The royal romance between Edward VIII and Wallis Simpson can now be seen through a unique new perspective: the eyes of those who spied on them, creating a secret dossier unlike any other.

programme.title: Spying on the Royals: Part 2
programme.desc: Newly declassified secret files reveal how King George VI and even the President of the United States ordered espionage on the former Edward VIII and Wallis Simpson, based on intelligence that Edward was attempting to usurp George VI.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: St Albans
programme.desc: Searching in prime commuter territory where property isn't cheap. Both couples have high expectations for their first homes and our duo will need all of their property know-how to come out on top!

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Yorkshire
programme.desc: This week we've been called in to help a lucky couple that have just scooped almost two million quid on the lottery and want to splash out on a swanky new pad.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Hackney
programme.desc: This week we're guiding two first time buyers through the property jungle that is East London. Rosie Evatt and Laura Coveney have been friends since childhood and are pooling their resources to buy their first property together.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Aberdeen City
programme.desc: Newlyweds Emma and Mike are bursting to move on and up.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Southbank
programme.desc: We have a teenager with an epic budget keen for independence; and a young couple with high hopes that their funds will stretch to the dream flat. Kirstie and Phil are on their home turf –can they secure a famous victory?

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Jersey
programme.desc: This week we're on Jersey hunting for a fabulous family home for the young couple Nick and Katrina. We show them the many highs and lows this Channel Island has to offer.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Surrey
programme.desc: This week Kirstie and Phil are in Surrey - and at opposite ends of the property ladder.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Hastings
programme.desc: This week Kirstie and Phil are in Hastings, and they've got a stormy search ahead of them. Life's a beach as our intrepid property experts try to find the dream home for two sets of tricky house-hunters.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Warwickshire & Chelmsford
programme.desc: This week we're catching up with two couples whose extensive requirements almost drove us to distraction.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Tufnell Park
programme.desc: This week Kirstie and Phil almost come to blows when they take on the impossible – finding a flat with private parking in pricey North London for £290,000. They're helping Laura Cream, a very single-minded young lady.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Bucks & Aberdeen Revisit
programme.desc: In Buckinghamshire, Emma and Andrew had been looking for the ideal family home, whilst in Aberdeenshire Karen and Russell needed to cut down their daily commute.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Windsor Revisit
programme.desc: About two years ago, first time buyers asked us to guide them. I've come back to see whether or not they lived happily.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Windsor
programme.desc: This week Kirstie and Phil are in Windsor -with two sets of well-traveled first time buyers

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: London Northside
programme.desc: Kirstie's house hunter is London born and bred and has viewed a whopping 90 properties already-whilst Phil's couple have yet to view a single place and have no idea where to buy.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Revisit 2: Jersey/Warwickshire
programme.desc: This week we're going back to visit two very different sets of house hunters who had one thing on their minds – space, and they wanted lots of it!

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Yorkshire & Guildford
programme.desc: After winning one point eight million on the lottery, they needed a new house.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Bath
programme.desc: This week we're back catching up with Sadie Yukon and Simon Gwillian, a picky pair who really put us through the mill.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Cheltenham And Gloucestershire
programme.desc: Cosmopolitan couple Mel and Jon have lived in cities all over the world and now want to settle into rural life in the Cotswolds.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Glasgow
programme.desc: In Glasgow is first time buyer project manager and property obsessive Adam. With a budget of £250,000, he's looked at 500 properties in the 9 months!

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Wicklow
programme.desc: This week we're helping a young mother achieve her dream of moving back to Ireland. Sue Wessell longs to return to the place where she grew up and has persuaded husband Chris and their two children to up sticks and relocate to the Emerald Isle.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Guildford
programme.desc: This week Kirstie and Phil are in Surrey on a quest to find dream homes for two sets of frustrated house hunters. Two very different budgets and briefs but with the property market raging it looks like they've taken on double the trouble.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Yorkshire
programme.desc: This week, we're back in Yorkshire catching up with lotto millionaires Roger and Lara Griffiths. Just over a year ago, this lucky pair scooped almost two million quid on the lottery and were desperate to splash out on a swanky new pad.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: South Bucks
programme.desc: With both sets of house hunters determined to have it all, Kirstie and Phil are going to have to use all the tricks of the trade if they're going to help them move forward and hopefully into the house of their dreams!

programme.title: Great British Menu: Central Fish
programme.desc: The chefs from the Central region– Sue Ellis, Richard Bainbridge, and Aktar Islam pull out all the stops with their fish dishes, hoping to impress former champion, Glynn Purnell.

programme.title: Great British Menu: Central Main
programme.desc: The chefs from the Central region battle it out with their main courses. Aktar Islam, Sue Ellis, and Richard Bainbridge are determined to win but which dish will take the poll position?

programme.title: Great British Menu: Central Dessert
programme.desc: It's the last chance for the chefs from the Central region to impress former champion, Glynn Purnell, as today the one with the lowest total score from across the week will leave the competition.

programme.title: Great British Menu: Central Judging
programme.desc: It's decision time on Great British Menu for the chefs from Central region. One chef left the competition and only two remain, as they cook their entire menus for the Great British Menu judges, Prue Leith, Matthew Fort, and Oliver Peyton.

programme.title: Great British Menu: South West Starter
programme.desc: It's the South West chefs battling it out – John Hooker, Paul Ainsworth, and Andre Garrett. Former Great British Menu champion Michael Caines will decide which dishes deserve the highest scores. The chefs begin with their starters.

programme.title: Great British Menu: South West Fish
programme.desc: The chefs from the South West region– Paul Ainsworth, John Hooker, and Andre Garrett pull out all the stops with their fish dishes, hoping to impress former champion, Michael Caines.

programme.title: Great British Menu: South West Main
programme.desc: The chefs from the South West battle it out with their main courses. Andre Garrett, Paul Ainsworth, and John Hooker are determined to win but which dish will take the poll position?

programme.title: Great British Menu: South West Dessert
programme.desc: It's the last chance for the chefs from the South West to impress former champion Michael Caines, as today the one with the lowest total score from across the week will leave the competition.

programme.title: Great British Menu: South West Judging
programme.desc: It's decision time on Great British Menu for the chefs from South West. One chef left the competition and only two remain, as they cook their entire menus for the Great British Menu judges, Prue Leith, Matthew Fort, and Oliver Peyton.

programme.title: Great British Menu: Scotland Starter
programme.desc: It's the Scotland chefs battling it out – Michael Smith, Philip Carnegie, and Tony Singh. The chefs begin with their starters, in a bid to get their menu through to the judges tasting.

programme.title: Great British Menu: Scotland Fish
programme.desc: The chefs from Scotland – Tony Singh, Michael Smith, and Philip Carnegie pull out all the stops with their fish dishes, hoping to impress veteran chef Alan Murchison.

programme.title: Great British Menu: Scotland Main
programme.desc: The chefs from Scotland battle it out with their main courses. Philip Carnegie, Tony Singh, and Michael Smith are determined to win but which dish will take the poll position?

programme.title: Great British Menu: Scotland Dessert
programme.desc: It's the last chance for the chefs from Scotland to impress veteran chef Alan Murchison. Michael Smith, Tony Singh, and Philip Carnegie deliver their desserts but which dish will earn top marks?

programme.title: Great British Menu: Scotland Judging
programme.desc: It's decision time on Great British Menu for the chefs from Scotland. One chef left the competition and only two remain, as they cook their entire menus for the Great British Menu judges, Prue Leith, Matthew Fort, and Oliver Peyton.

programme.title: Great British Menu: Wales Starter
programme.desc: It's the Welsh chefs battling it out – Aled Williams, Gareth Jones, and Hywel Jones, with Angela Hartnett deciding who will get through to the judges on Friday The chefs begin with their starters.

programme.title: Great British Menu: Wales Fish
programme.desc: The chefs from Wales – Aled Williams, Gareth Jones and Hywel Jones pull out all the stops with their fish dishes, hoping to impress veteran chef Angela Hartnett.

programme.title: Great British Menu: Wales Main
programme.desc: The chefs from Wales battle it out with their main courses. Aled Williams, Gareth Jones and Hywel Jones are determined to win but which dish will take poll position?

programme.title: Great British Menu: Wales Dessert
programme.desc: It's the last chance for the chefs from Wales to impress veteran chef Angela Hartnett, as the one with the lowest total score from across the week will leave the competition. Aled Williams, Gareth Jones and Hywel Jones deliver their desserts.

programme.title: Great British Menu: Scotland Starter
programme.desc: Great British Menu is back and the fight is on as the nation's greatest chefs battle for the chance to cook a glittering Olympic Banquet. It's the battle of the Scottish chefs – returning chef Alan Murchison and newcomers Colin Buchan and Mark Greenaway.

programme.title: Great British Menu: Scotland Fish
programme.desc: Scottish chefs - Alan Murchison, Colin Buchan, and Mark Greenaway cook their fish course, but which one will prevail? Will it be Grilled Mackerel with beetroot meringues and horseradish ice cream; Crab 'cannelloni' or poached halibut with squid ink?

programme.title: Great British Menu: Scotland Main
programme.desc: It's the battle of the Scottish chefs – Alan Murchison, Colin Buchan, and Mark Greenaway. Today is the big one, the main course, but which one will get top marks?

programme.title: Great British Menu: Scotland Dessert
programme.desc: It's the battle of the Scottish chefs – Alan Murchison, Colin Buchan, and Mark Greenaway. Today is the final chance to impress the veteran with their dessert dishes.

programme.title: Great British Menu: Scotland Judging
programme.desc: It's decision time on Great British Menu for the chefs from Scotland. One chef left the competition and only two remain, who will cook their entire menus for the Great British Menu judges, Prue Leith, Matthew Fort, and Oliver Peyton.

programme.desc: It's the battle of the Central chefs – returning contenders, Aktar Islam and Daniel Clifford and newcomer Paul Foster. They kick things off with their starters, but which one is going to be first off the blocks?

programme.title: Running Wild With Bear Grylls: Bear Grylls, Kate Hudson
programme.desc: Oscar-nominated actress Kate Hudson goes on a journey with Bear that involves a treacherous mountain range in Italy; Kate shares details of her unique upbringing.

programme.title: Running Wild With Bear Grylls: Bear Grylls, Zac Efron
programme.desc: Hollywood actor Zac Efron heads into the Appalachian wilderness of the Catskill Mountains in New York. They parachute into the woods, rappel down cliffs and search for shelter.

programme.title: Running Wild With Bear Grylls: Bear Grylls, Don Cheadle
programme.desc: Actor Don Cheadle joins Bear on a trek through the mountains of New England, which includes crossing a raging river and a climb to a towering railroad bridge. Bear considers a porcupine carcass for dinner.

programme.title: Running Wild With Bear Grylls: Bear Grylls, Courtney Cox
programme.desc: Actress Courteney Cox joins Bear to challenge herself into sharp edged discomfort amidst the jagged peaks and descending cliffs on the windswept coast of Ireland.

programme.title: Running Wild With Bear Grylls: Bear Grylls, Kate Winslett
programme.desc: Acclaimed movie star Kate Winslet takes a role like never before, as Bear paramotors them both to the top of the Snowdonia Mountains in Wales.

programme.title: Running Wild With Bear Grylls: Bear Grylls, Derek Hough
programme.desc: Dancer Derek Hough joins Bear on an adventure across the mountains of Bulgaria; Derek reveals childhood struggles and teaches Bear some dance moves.

programme.title: Running Wild With Bear Grylls: Bear Grylls, Sterling K. Brown
programme.desc: Actor Sterling K. Brown joins Bear in the high mountains of Colorado, where they battle plummeting temperatures while descending a sheer rock face, track an elusive deer and rappel down a waterfall.

programme.title: Running Wild With Bear Grylls: Bear Grylls, Michael B. Jordan
programme.desc: Actor Michael B. Jordan joins Grylls in Wales where they start their journey by dropping into rough waters off the coast before making their way up the coast and to the top of the mountains for their exit.

programme.title: Running Wild With Bear Grylls: Bear Grylls, Lindsey Vonn
programme.desc: Bear and Olympic gold medallist Lindsey Vonn take a speedboat trip, then swim to shore and make their way along a rugged coastline; after traversing a deep chasm, they collect a dinner of sea urchins.

programme.title: Running Wild With Bear Grylls: Bear Grylls, Vanessa Hudgens
programme.desc: Bear and Vanessa Hudgens rappel from a helicopter into the high Sierra for a scenic adventure; Bear and Vanessa evade a boulder avalanche, summit a massive dormant volcano, and encounter a rattlesnake; Vanessa talks about her rise to stardom.

programme.title: Running Wild With Bear Grylls: Bear Grylls, James Marsden
programme.desc: X-man' James Marsden joins Bear on an adventure into the heart Rattle Snake Canyon in Utah, where they helicopter on to one of the highest arches in the area.

programme.title: Running Wild With Bear Grylls: Bear Grylls, Marshawn Lynch
programme.desc: Bear and Super Bowl champion Marshawn Lynch tackle a rugged mountain range that has sharp cliffs and dark chasms; the pair comes face to face with the French Foreign Legion and pursues a wild hog for dinner.

programme.title: Running Wild With Bear Grylls: Bear Grylls, Roger Federer
programme.desc: Bear takes one of the best athletes of all time, Roger Federer, on a daring adventure through the Swiss Alps as they attempt to navigate the tough terrain. They descend a frozen waterfall and use tennis rackets as snowshoes.

programme.title: Running Wild With Bear Grylls: Bear Grylls, Nick Jonas
programme.desc: Renowned survivalist Bear Grylls is accompanied by singer Nick Jonas on another one of his journeys into the wilderness.

programme.title: Running Wild With Bear Grylls: Bear Grylls, Joseph Gordon-Levitt
programme.desc: Bear and Joseph Gordon-Levitt navigate Kenya's unyielding terrain; Joseph and Bear navigate a perilous rappel, milk a camel for hydration, ascend a massive rock dome and battle a crocodile for dinner; Joseph talks about his family and rise to fame.

programme.title: Running Wild With Bear Grylls: Bear Grylls, Julianne Hough
programme.desc: Bear and actress Julianne Hough navigate the unforgiving terrain and wild animals of the African savanna; Julianne talks about her adolescence and her determination to succeed.

programme.title: Running Wild With Bear Grylls: Bear Grylls, Tom Arnold
programme.desc: Tom Arnold heads into the unpredictable coastal mountains of Oregon. They take a helicopter to an isolated forest, hike through woodland, catch a trout and cross a river with the help of a log.

programme.title: Running Wild With Bear Grylls: Bear Grylls, Julia Roberts
programme.desc: Movie star Julia Roberts joins Bear on a lifesaving Red Nose Day adventure in Kenya. They're on a two-day trek enduring blistering savannah temperatures.

programme.title: Running Wild With Bear Grylls: Bear Grylls, Deon Sanders
programme.desc: Bear Grylls takes NFL hall of famer Deion Sanders to the mesas and canyons of Utah for his first ever camping trip.

programme.title: Running Wild With Bear Grylls: Bear Grylls, Ben Stiller
programme.desc: Ben Stiller travels to Northern Scotland to face the Isle of Skye's rugged mountains. Stiller discusses his background while they navigate slippery terrain, searching for food and clean water.
